description: Complete week showing all app features in action

priorities: 2. Violin practice: focus on chord progression from last lesson
3. Respond to coach about gymnastics schedule
output.socratic_prompt: You have a CS quiz today. If you were to fail it, what would be the most likely cause? [Inversion Framework]
output.user_response: Probably forgot edge cases in recursive decomposition. I'll review those specifically.
output.ai_feedback: Edge case identified. Connecting to your gymnastics training: just as you check landing surface before a flip, check base cases before recursion. Cross-domain link created! +50 XP

input.subject: Computational Thinking
input.content: Professor explained recursion using Russian Matryoshka dolls - each doll contains a smaller version of itself until you reach the base case (smallest doll)

ai_processing: Personal Agent: Extracted core concept - Recursion as self-similarity
Research Agent: Enriched with examples from nature (fractals, trees, coastlines)
Synthesis Agent: Connected to Music (self-similar melodies), Art (fractals), Biology (cell division)
Evaluation Agent: Verified understanding with quick check

knowledge_graph_additions.cross_domain_analogy: Recursion is like a song that contains a shorter version of its own chorus

events.content: Saw tree branches splitting - exactly like recursion! Each branch splits into smaller versions.

ai_response.action: Universal Mind Map updated with Nature→CS connection
ai_response.socratic_prompt: If recursion happens in trees (nature), music (canons), and code, where ELSE might you find it? Think about your gymnastics routines...
ai_response.user_response: Combo moves! A flip contains a smaller rotation within it, and you repeat with variations. The base case is the landing!

coach_questions: Best decision today? Connecting recursion to gymnastics - helped me understand both better.
Mental model used? Pattern Recognition + Transfer Learning.
Cross-domain insight of the day? Recursion appears in CS, Nature, Music, Gymnastics - it's a UNIVERSAL pattern.
events.ai_summary: Today you discovered recursion as a universal pattern across 4 domains. Your knowledge graph has grown by 5 nodes and 8 connections. You've practiced Transfer Learning actively. +150 XP for reflection.

input.content: Skittles trading game - teacher took 1 skittle per trade as commission. Nobody asked why we were doing this. Teacher revealed: He's the trade commissioner, benefits without competing. This is how credit card companies work!

ai_processing.research_enrichment: Credit card interchange fees: 1.5-3.5% per transaction. Visa/Mastercard revenue: $30B+ annually from fees alone.
ai_processing.mental_model_applied: Inversion: 'Why didn't anyone ask WHY we were doing this?' → Authority bias prevented questioning
ai_processing.synthesis_agent: Connect to CS: APIs are middlemen between services. They extract value (data, fees) from transactions. Same pattern!

events.scenario: Buying gift for girlfriend. Remembered credit card fees from class.
events.decision: Use cash instead of card to avoid transaction fees

ai_response.praise: Excellent transfer! You applied Business class learning to personal finance.
ai_response.mental_model: Circle of Competence (you understood fees) + Opportunity Cost (cash vs card benefits)

events.ai_detection: User has argument-related note from yesterday
events.coach_prompt: You have a group project meeting tomorrow. Yesterday you noted tension with a teammate. Want to do a quick pre-mortem? [5 min]

pre_mortem_output.imagined_failure: Project failed because we couldn't agree on approach
prevention_actions: Use audience adaptation framework: understand teammate's perspective first
Apply steelmanning: strengthen their argument before presenting yours
Propose small test rather than big commitment

events.user_action: Used English class audience adaptation + steelmanning from last night
events.outcome: Teammate felt heard, compromise reached, positive outcome

ai_response.analysis: You combined Communication skill (audience adaptation) with Business framework (negotiation) and Mental Model (steelmanning). Triple cross-domain application!
ai_response.badge: First successful steelmanning in real life!

events.action: Opened Knowledge Map, saw Elena (Psychology PhD) 200m away studying cognitive load
events.interaction: Sent knowledge collision request: 'Your cognitive load research + my CS learning = ?'
events.outcome: 5-minute voice chat. Elena explained chunking in working memory. Connected to Alex's decomposition concept!
events.ai_synthesis: New insight: Decomposition (CS) = Chunking (Psychology) = Breaking information into manageable units. Same principle, different domain!
events.new_connection: Met Elena, added to study pod candidates

input.content: Chord progression diagram showing emotional journey. Major = happy/resolved, Minor = sad/tension, Suspended = anticipation

ai_processing.synthesis: Connect to Communication class: tone modulation creates emotional response. Music IS communication without words.
ai_processing.deeper_connection: Connect to Business: Customer journey has emotional arc like music. Tension → Resolution = Problem → Solution in sales.
ai_processing.universal_principle: Arc structures: Setup → Tension → Resolution appears in Music, Stories, Business pitches, Gymnastics routines

ai_generated_summary.key_insight: Recursion/Decomposition/Chunking is a UNIVERSAL pattern across nature, CS, psychology, and music. You've discovered a fundamental principle!
ai_generated_summary.recommended_next_week: Explore Sports/Physics domain to complete your 'universal pattern' with angular momentum in gymnastics

events.activity: Each person taught one concept in 5 minutes
events.alex_taught: Recursion as universal pattern (using tree branches as demo)
learned_from_others: Biologist: Fractal patterns in leaf veins
Dancer: Choreography as recursive movement building

ai_post_processing.community_validated_insight: Recursion universality confirmed by 3 independent domains!
